legalStructure: GFI is treated in this dataset as the Goldfinch protocol/governance token, while Goldfinch Prime and legacy Goldfinch credit pools should be researched as separate product or pool contexts if Nexus RWA later tracks them as investable private-credit assets.
targetInvestors: GFI: protocol participants, governance participants, and secondary-market token holders. Goldfinch Prime or other private-credit products may have separate eligibility, terms, and jurisdiction restrictions.

classification.classificationNote: GFI is the Goldfinch protocol/governance token. It is not a direct redeemable claim on a specific credit portfolio, fund share, note, tranche, or reserve-backed asset. Goldfinch Prime and other credit products should be modeled separately if tracked as investable RWA exposure.

metadata.productScopeNote: This layer is scoped to GFI as a protocol token. Goldfinch Prime, Heron-issued notes, and legacy Goldfinch V1 pools should be researched as separate product contexts if Nexus RWA later tracks them as investable private-credit assets.

goldfinchPrimeProductNotes.managerOrRelatedEntity: Heron entity referenced in official Goldfinch Prime FAQ; requires regulator verification before production grading.
goldfinchPrimeProductNotes.instrumentType: Contingent payment notes referenced in official Goldfinch Prime FAQ; product-level context only.
goldfinchPrimeProductNotes.underlyingExposure: Equity interests in third-party private-credit funds referenced in official Goldfinch Prime FAQ; product-level context only.

feesProductLevelOnly.notes: Official Goldfinch Prime FAQ references 0.5% annual management fee and 0.5% withdrawal fee. These are Goldfinch Prime product fees, not native GFI token fees.

dataGaps: Direct issuer/legal entity for GFI token not confirmed from a public legal document.
No GFI prospectus, offering memorandum, or legal opinion URL found in this review.
Heron/Warbler regulatory status should be verified through SEC IAPD/EDGAR before any institutional-grade classification.
Any live private-credit product exposure should be split into separate asset records rather than mapped directly to GFI.

_meta.notes: Migrated to the Nexus RWA classification-first standard. GFI is treated as a governance/protocol asset, so reserve, custody, redemption, and proof-of-reserves are not applicable rather than missing evidence.
